claim: In the Luthey-Schulten lab's chromosome simulations (Gilbert 2023, Thornburg 2026 4DWCM), the 543,379 bp JCVI-Syn3A chromosome is represented as a worm-like-chain polymer with ~54,300 beads at 10 bp per bead.
source_detail: Gilbert et al. 2023 establishes the 10-bp-per-bead WLC polymer with excluded volume + SMC-like loop extrusion + topoisomerase action. Thornburg 2026 inherits the model unchanged. Simulated in LAMMPS with Kokkos GPU acceleration (btree_chromo_gpu).
caveats: Modelling choice, not a measurement. The 10 bp / bead discretisation is a trade-off between polymer detail and BD simulation tractability.
Not directly relevant to our CPU-only essentiality predictor (which does not model chromosome topology). Recorded for citation completeness in case future work wants a chromosome representation.
Extracted from WebSearch snippets in Session 11.
